marketTone: Moonshot Kimi K3 (Jul 16) triggered a global chip de-risking: Jul 17 S&P −1.01% to 7,457.69, SOX 11,673.89, Shanghai −3.05% (broke 3800); Brent settled $88.10 on Iran/Kuwait escalation; sentiment 54.4 beat estimate but surveys largely pre-dated the oil spike.

summary.text: Jul 17 US close (CNBC): S&P −1.01% to 7,457.69, Nasdaq −1.4% to 25,520.24, Dow −0.77% to 52,146.42, Russell 2000 −0.4% to 2,962.22 — first losing week in three; PHLX SOX fell about 1.6% (Yahoo Finance) as Moonshot's Kimi K3 (2.8T params, Jul 16) revived 'DeepSeek 2.0' fears on AI capex.

Jul 17 A-share close (Xinhua): Shanghai −3.05% to 3,764.15 (broke 3800), Shenzhen −5.4%, ChiNext −7.15%, STAR −8.13%; Hang Seng −1.8% to 24,562 (The Standard). Nikkei 225 −4.03% to 64,141.12 entered correction territory (Yonhap Infomax) — global semi beta overwhelmed WAIC day-one headlines.

Jul 17 commodities/rates: Brent Sep futures settled +4.6% at $88.10, WTI Aug $82.49 (CNBC) after Kuwait said Iran struck a power/desalination plant; COMEX gold front-month settled $4,012.70 (Morningstar/Dow Jones); 10y Treasury ~4.55% (US Treasury curve, Jul 17).

Jul 17 data mix: preliminary Michigan sentiment 54.4 (beat estimate vs ~51.0, Michigan); June housing starts +19.0% m/m SAAR to 1.427M (beat vs ~12.2% expected, Census); June industrial production +0.1% m/m (miss estimate vs ~0.2%, Fed G.17) — resilient housing/sentiment overshadowed by Kimi/oil risk-off.

Jul 16–17 China AI: Moonshot released Kimi K3 (2.8T params, weights due Jul 27) ahead of WAIC (Jul 17–20, 第一财经); WAICO launched with 29 founding nations Jul 16 (Caixin); BBC Jul 17 noted Zhipu −~27% and MiniMax −~16% in Hong Kong on competitive fears.

signal: Kimi K3 re-priced global AI capex — Jul 17 SOX −1.6% (bear-market territory) and Shanghai −3.05% synced while Brent $88.10 kept hawkish Fed/oil risk alive despite sentiment and housing beats.
watch: Does SOX hold above 11,500 through next week's mega-cap earnings, or does Kimi K3's Jul 27 open-weight release extend the semi bear-market unwind?
globalRegime: Fed holds 3.50%–3.75% with a speaker split (Williams dovish Jul 15 vs Logan/Jefferson hawkish Jul 16). Jul 17 brought Kimi K3-driven chip de-risking (SOX 11,673.89), Brent $88.10 on Iran/Kuwait escalation, and mixed macro (sentiment 54.4 beat, housing starts 1.427M beat, IP +0.1% miss). Jul 17 US indexes posted weekly losses; Asia synced lower with Shanghai −3.05% and Nikkei −4.03%.

globalChanged.text: Jul 17 US official close (CNBC): S&P −1.01% to 7,457.69, Nasdaq −1.4% to 25,520.24, Dow −0.77% to 52,146.42, Russell 2000 −0.4% to 2,962.22; weekly S&P −1.6%, Nasdaq −2.9%, Dow −0.9%.
Jul 17 PHLX SOX fell about 1.6% (Yahoo Finance close); Philadelphia semiconductor index entered bear market territory, down 20.2% from its Jun 22 record close (Straits Times Jul 18).
Moonshot Kimi K3 (Jul 16): 2.8T-parameter model; open weights due Jul 27; Arena.ai frontend-code #1 score — chip selloff linked to AI capex ROI fears (VentureBeat Jul 16, BBC Jul 17).

Jul 17 rates/commodities: Brent Sep settled $88.10 (+4.6%), WTI Aug $82.49 (+4.5%) after Kuwait reported Iran attack on desalination/power plant (CNBC); COMEX gold settled $4,012.70 (Morningstar/Dow Jones); 10y Treasury ~4.55% (US Treasury curve, Jul 17).

Jul 17 preliminary Michigan sentiment 54.4 vs 49.5 prior (beat estimate ~51.0); >70% of interviews completed before Jul 7 Iran strikes/oil spike (Michigan). June housing starts SAAR 1.427M (+19.0% m/m, beat ~12.2% expected); single-family starts 895k (−0.2% m/m) — multi-family drove headline (Census, Jul 17).
June industrial production +0.1% m/m (Fed G.17, Jul 17) — miss estimate vs ~0.2%; manufacturing unchanged, mining/utilities +0.4% each.
Jul 17 Nikkei 225 close −4.03% to 64,141.12 (correction territory, −11.4% from Jun 25 record; Yonhap Infomax); Kospi closed for Constitution Day holiday.

globalImplies.text: Kimi K3 shifts the AI falsifier from demand scarcity to model-efficiency/capex ROI — Jul 17 chip selloff hit US, Japan, and China simultaneously despite strong early Q2 earnings beats.
Sentiment 54.4 and housing starts 1.427M beat are backward-looking vs Jul 17 Brent $88.10 — oil re-acceleration can revive hawkish Fed pricing that CPI/PPI misses had closed.
Multi-family–led housing starts beat does not repair single-family −0.2% m/m — rate-sensitive housing pipeline still soft alongside IP +0.1% miss.

globalTensions: Kimi K3 efficiency vs AI capex narrative; sentiment/housing beats vs Brent $88.10; Williams dovish Jul 15 vs Logan/Jefferson hawkish Jul 16; Jul 17 Shanghai −3.05% vs WAIC/WAICO launch; gold $4,012.70 vs oil spike.
chinaStance: Showcase-and-governance heavy — WAIC (Jul 17–20) and WAICO (29 nations, Jul 16) highlight AI leadership, but Jul 17 onshore plunge (−3.05% Shanghai) shows global semi beta still dominates local policy headlines; no new PBOC price-easing signal in the coverage window.

chinaChanged.text: Jul 17 A-share close (Xinhua): Shanghai −3.05% to 3,764.15 (broke 3800), Shenzhen −5.4% to 13,706.88, ChiNext −7.15% to 3,428.63, STAR −8.13%; turnover 2.65tn yuan; ~5001 stocks fell, ~198 limit-down (Sina wrap).
Jul 17 Hang Seng close −1.8% to 24,562 (The Standard); Hang Seng Tech −4.4%; Z.ai −28.5%, SMIC −10% amid Kimi-driven model-stock selloff.

Moonshot Kimi K3 (Jul 16, 华尔街见闻 3777138): 2.8T-parameter open-weight flagship; weights due Jul 27; API $3/$15 per million tokens; timed ahead of WAIC — markets read as competitive threat to US closed-model premium.
WAIC Jul 17–20 opens in Shanghai — 140+ forums, 1100+ exhibitors, 300+ global debuts (第一财经 Jul 17); WAICO established Jul 16 with 29 founding nations, HQ Shanghai (Caixin Jul 17).
BlockBeats (Jul 17): Base Camp launched The Base App (Coinbase Wallet upgrade) with Flashblocks on mainnet — L2 shifts from infrastructure to integrated super-app entry.
Jul 16 A-share close (京报网): Shanghai −1.85% to 3882.41, Shenzhen −1.97%, ChiNext −2.95%; storage/semis led declines.

chinaImplies.text: Jul 17 onshore and offshore both sold on Kimi K3 read-through — WAIC/WAICO governance headlines failed to offset SOX/Nikkei drag; model-stock losers (Z.ai −28.5%) dominated vs infrastructure winners.
Kimi K3 open weights Jul 27 is the next falsifier for both China AI names and global semi capex — until then, markets trade positioning not benchmarks.
A/H split from Jul 16 (HSI +1.33% vs Shanghai −1.85%) closed Jul 17 with both indices falling — global beta unified the complex.

chinaDivergences: Jul 16 WAIC optimism vs Jul 17 Shanghai −3.05%; Kimi K3 China launch vs US chip selloff; sentiment 54.4 beat (pre-oil) vs Brent $88.10; Jul 16 Hang Seng +1.33% vs Jul 17 −1.8% as A/H beta converged lower.

assetFramework.regime: AI capex ROI vs Fed path — Kimi K3 efficiency shock vs oil/hawkish Fed speak
assetFramework.driver: Jul 17 S&P −1.01% / Nasdaq −1.4%; SOX −1.6%; Kimi K3 Jul 16; Brent $88.10 Jul 17
assetFramework.read: Cap-weighted chip drag drove Jul 17 weekly loss — narrow AI leadership crack, not broad macro collapse (sentiment/housing beat)
assetFramework.invalidator: SOX reclaims 12,400 with S&P positive two sessions while Brent settles <$82

assetFramework.regime: Inflation prints vs oil premium + Fed speaker split
assetFramework.driver: 10y ~4.55% Jul 17; Brent $88.10; IP +0.1% miss; sentiment 54.4 beat (stale vs oil)
assetFramework.read: Yields eased slightly Jul 17 despite oil spike — disinflation week still in memory, but Brent $88 reopens term-premium risk
assetFramework.invalidator: 10y breaks above 4.65% with Brent >$88 for three settles

assetFramework.regime: Rate differentials with a Hormuz safe-haven bid
assetFramework.driver: Brent $88.10 Jul 17; Iran/Kuwait escalation; resilient housing/sentiment data Jul 17
assetFramework.read: Oil/geo bid likely offset soft-inflation relief — no decisive DXY break verified at Jul 17 close
assetFramework.invalidator: DXY falls while Brent >$88 and 10y also rises — pure haven bid questioned

assetFramework.regime: Real-yield beta tugged by oil-driven inflation fears
assetFramework.read: Held ~$4,000 but −2.23% on the week — oil inflation fears capped CPI-relief bounce
assetFramework.invalidator: Gold reclaims $4,100 while 10y also rises — pure geo-hedge regime

assetFramework.regime: Geopolitical supply risk — Hormuz/Kuwait infrastructure premium dominates
assetFramework.driver: Brent Sep settled $88.10 Jul 17 (+4.6%); Kuwait desalination plant strike; US 6th consecutive night of Iran strikes (CNBC)
assetFramework.read: Supply-risk beta overwrote Jul 16 session dip — forward inflation input for Fed path
assetFramework.invalidator: Brent settles below $82 for three sessions with Hormuz crossings recovering

assetFramework.regime: Global semi beta vs domestic AI showcase — Kimi K3 shock dominates
assetFramework.driver: Shanghai −3.05% Jul 17 to 3764.15 (Xinhua); Hang Seng −1.8%; WAIC opens Jul 17; Kimi K3 Jul 16
assetFramework.read: Jul 17 plunge synced with SOX/Nikkei — WAIC day-one failed to offset global chip unwind; A/H split closed
assetFramework.invalidator: Shanghai reclaims 3,900 on rising volume while SOX stabilizes above 12,000

assetFramework.regime: PBOC fix as policy signal; no new price-easing in coverage window
assetFramework.driver: Jul 17 fix 6.7934 (MNI, Jul 17 briefing); Jul 17–18 no new OMO/LPR action in window
assetFramework.read: Last verified fix weaker than survey Jul 17 — mild depreciation tolerance; weekend pause
assetFramework.invalidator: Fix persistently weaker than model estimates for a week with DR007 rising

assetFramework.regime: High-beta global liquidity asset in the oil→Fed→semi chain
assetFramework.driver: Jul 17 BTC ~$63,000 (CoinDesk/Cointelegraph) after Kimi K3 chip selloff
assetFramework.read: Gave back PPI-relief gains — tracks semi/oil risk-off, not a safe-haven decouple
assetFramework.invalidator: BTC holds $65k while SOX falls another −3% — would break semi-beta frame

signals.name: Kimi K3 triggers global AI capex re-pricing
signals.evidence: Moonshot Kimi K3 2.8T params launched Jul 16 (VentureBeat/BBC); Jul 17 SOX −1.6%, S&P −1.01%, Nikkei −4.03%, Shanghai −3.05%; Z.ai −28.5% H-share (BBC/The Standard).
signals.mechanism: Low-cost open-weight frontier model revives DeepSeek-style fears on US AI ROI — crowded semi/AI longs de-risk globally, not just in China.
signals.disprovedIf: SOX reclaims 12,400 within five sessions and Kimi K3 open-weight release Jul 27 draws muted market reaction.

signals.name: Jul 17 onshore plunge syncs with global semi de-risking
signals.evidence: Shanghai −3.05% to 3764.15, ChiNext −7.15%, STAR −8.13% Jul 17 (Xinhua); follows Jul 16 −1.85%; Hang Seng −1.8% Jul 17 (The Standard).
signals.mechanism: WAIC/WAICO headlines insufficient vs SOX/Nikkei drag — onshore beta tracks global chip positioning, not local showcase alone.
signals.disprovedIf: Shanghai reclaims 3,900 within three sessions while SOX stays below 12,000.

signals.name: Brent $88.10 re-accelerates inflation/Fed hawk case
signals.evidence: Brent settled $88.10 Jul 17 (+4.6%) after Kuwait infrastructure strike report (CNBC); follows Jul 16 $84.23 settle.
signals.mechanism: Forward energy inflation overwrites backward-looking CPI/PPI misses and stale sentiment survey — arms Logan/Jefferson hawkish frame.
signals.disprovedIf: Brent settles below $82 for three sessions with no new Gulf infrastructure strikes.

signals.name: Mixed macro — sentiment/housing beat, IP miss
signals.evidence: Michigan sentiment 54.4 beat ~51.0 (Jul 17); housing starts 1.427M +19% m/m beat (Census); IP +0.1% m/m miss vs ~0.2% (Fed G.17).
signals.mechanism: Consumer/housing resilience vs manufacturing flatline — limits how much Kimi/oil selloff can claim a growth scare, but does not stop chip unwind.
signals.disprovedIf: Next IP print ≥0.3% m/m with manufacturing +0.2% while SOX also stabilizes.

signals.name: Nikkei enters correction on chip rout
signals.evidence: Nikkei 225 −4.03% to 64,141.12 Jul 17, −11.4% from Jun 25 record (Yonhap Infomax); Kioxia −16% intraday (Phemex/Yonhap).
signals.mechanism: Asia leveraged semi beta amplifies US Kimi shock — correction territory raises regional margin-call risk into Jul 28–29 FOMC.
signals.disprovedIf: Nikkei reclaims 66,800 within five sessions with SOX >12,000.

signals.name: WAIC/WAICO showcase vs market selloff
signals.evidence: WAIC Jul 17–20 (第一财经); WAICO 29 nations Jul 16 (Caixin); Shanghai −3.05% Jul 17 (Xinhua).
signals.mechanism: Policy/showcase cycle lands but global semi de-risking dominates tape — Jul 17 plunge confirms.
signals.disprovedIf: STAR 50 outperforms SOX by >5pp over five WAIC sessions while Shanghai holds 3,850+.

watchItems.headline: SOX bear-market band / Kimi K3 Jul 27 open-weight release
watchItems.why: STRONG Kimi K3 capex-repricing signal — Jul 17 weekly chip loss.
watchItems.watch: SOX vs Jul 17 −1.6% close (Yahoo Finance); mega-cap earnings cluster next week; Kimi weights Jul 27.
watchItems.trigger: SOX closes below 11,500 with S&P also −1% — broadens from narrow AI crack to index risk-off.
watchItems.invalidator: SOX reclaims 12,400 and Nasdaq positive two straight sessions before Jul 27 weights drop.

watchItems.headline: Jul 17 onshore depth — Shanghai 3764.15 / broke 3800
watchItems.why: STRONG onshore sync signal with global semi beta.
watchItems.watch: Shanghai vs 3764.15; STAR vs 1938.77; turnover vs 2.65tn yuan Jul 17.
watchItems.trigger: Shanghai closes below 3,700 with turnover >2.8tn yuan.
watchItems.invalidator: Shanghai reclaims 3,900 within three sessions with SOX stabilizing above 12,000.

watchItems.headline: Brent $88.10 / Hormuz-Kuwait escalation band
watchItems.why: STRONG energy-premium signal feeding Fed hawk case.
watchItems.watch: Brent daily settle (Jul 17: $88.10); Kuwait/Gulf infrastructure headlines; 10y vs 4.55%.
watchItems.trigger: Brent settles above $90 for two sessions or Kuwait reports second infrastructure strike.
watchItems.invalidator: Brent settle below $82 for five sessions with Hormuz crossings recovering.

watchItems.headline: Mega-cap earnings vs Kimi narrative
watchItems.why: MODERATE macro resilience signal — early Q2 beats did not protect Jul 17 multiples.
watchItems.watch: Google/Tesla/Intel/SAP reports next week; management AI capex commentary vs Kimi pricing.
watchItems.trigger: Two+ megacaps guide down AI spend or cut capex — validates Kimi falsifier.
watchItems.invalidator: Two+ megacaps raise AI capex with stable margins and SOX reclaims 12,200.

watchItems.headline: Contrarian — stale sentiment 54.4 vs live oil
watchItems.why: Michigan survey >70% pre-Jul 7 Iran/oil spike — beat may not reflect Jul 17 tape.
watchItems.watch: Final Michigan Jul 31; gas prices vs Jul 13 interview window; consumer discretionary vs energy Jul 17 close.
watchItems.trigger: Final sentiment falls below 50 with gas >$3.50/gal national average while equities also fall.
watchItems.invalidator: Final sentiment holds ≥54 with core PCE ≤0.2% m/m and Brent <$85.

sources: CNBC (Jul 17 US close; Brent/WTI settle), Yahoo Finance (SOX Jul 17), VentureBeat/BBC/华尔街见闻 (Kimi K3 Jul 16–17), University of Michigan (Jul sentiment), US Census Bureau (Jun housing starts Jul 17), Federal Reserve G.17 (Jun IP Jul 17), US Treasury yield curve, Morningstar/Dow Jones (COMEX gold Jul 17), CoinDesk + Cointelegraph (BTC Jul 17), Yonhap Infomax (Nikkei Jul 17), Xinhua (Jul 17 A-share close), The Standard (Hang Seng Jul 17), Caixin Global (WAICO Jul 17), 第一财经/Yicai (WAIC Jul 17), BlockBeats (Base Jul 17), 京报网 (Jul 16 A-share close).
singleSource: Rejected AP Jul 17 S&P 7,475.69 (typo vs CNBC/consensus 7,457.69). Rejected 华尔街见闻 3751205/3751275 (2025 stale wraps). Rejected wallstreetcn 3772569 Trump Iran delay (weekday/date inconsistency in page text — omitted). Dropped Sina Jul 17 wrap as secondary to Xinhua for index levels. Michigan survey timing caveat (>70% pre-Jul 7 Iran strikes) noted in signals/watch.
